name: Paul Horn
description: American flautist in jazz and New Age music (1930-2014)
abstract: Paul Horn (March 17, 1930 – June 29, 2014) was an American flautist, saxophonist, composer and producer. He became a pioneer of world and new age music with his 1969 album Inside.

concerts.label: Dick Schory and His Percussion Pops Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: one of my early groups, when Buddy Collette left the group, Paul Horn joined the group... Carson Smith, Paul Horn, John Pisano and Fred Katz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 30, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: You went through Paul Horn, Eric Dolphy... Somebody recommended him, you know, and I heard him and, you know, gave him a shot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t teach Paul but he was a good friend of mine, we have a lot in common
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Holly Hofmann
about_quotes.evidence: People gave me a lot of Paul Horn and Herbie Mann and you know my parents’ friends gave me a wealth of LPs and things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holly Hofmann interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...Bud Shank, Paul Horn. ... Any recollection about that one? [INTERVIEWEE] Does it say who it's for?
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
